data.title: South Korea's Stablecoin Flow Shows Balanced Capital Movement
data.description: According to PANews, the South Korean Financial Services Commission (FSC) has stated that dollar-denominated stablecoins do not lead to unilateral capital outflows. Data from the FSC reveals that between November 2024 and February 2025, the total amount of dollar stablecoins entering South Korea reached 35.3 trillion won (approximately $24.8 billion), with an equivalent amount flowing out of the country. This marks the first time the FSC has disclosed data on cross-border stablecoin movements.

data.title: Malta's Financial Authority Warns of Scam Targeting Cryptocurrency Traders
data.description:
According to PANews, the Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA) has issued a public warning about scammers who are forging documents from the Maltese financial regulator to demand money from traders under the guise of fictitious market manipulation fines.

This scam primarily targets Bitcoin and Ethereum traders. The MFSA reported that fraudsters are sending fake documents to individuals involved in BTC and ETH trading, falsely claiming they are fined for alleged market manipulation and threatening regulatory action if they do not comply. The MFSA has confirmed that these threats are entirely fabricated and that the documents hold no legal validity. The counterfeit letters are meticulously crafted to mimic the MFSA's logo in an attempt to deceive recipients into making payments.

data.title: Three addresses bought "Base is for everyone" tokens in advance and cashed out a total of about $666,000
OKX Wallet independent app is officially available on the App Store
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the OKX Wallet independent application is now available on Google Play and Apple App Store in some regions. Users can directly search for "OKX Wallet" to download and experience it. You can also click the link to download: https://web3.okx.com/zh-hans/download
It is reported that OKX Wallet is the industry's leading one-stop Web3 portal, which now supports more than 130 popular public chains.

data.title: Ethereum Gas Fees Reach Lowest Level Since 2020
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Etherscan indicates that Ethereum's gas fees have dropped to 0.358 Gwei, marking the lowest level since May 2, 2020. The current estimated cost for a swap transaction is $0.21.

data.title: Galaxy Digital has transferred a total of 49,681 ETH to CEX in the past five days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to @spotonchain monitoring, Galaxy Digital transferred 12,181 ETH to Coinbase about 8 hours ago during the ETH callback, which is about 19.02 million US dollars at the current price. In the past 5 days, the institution has transferred a total of 49,681 ETH to centralized exchanges, with a total value of approximately 79.36 million US dollars, and the average transfer price is $1,597.

data.title: Significant Outflow of FBTC Observed, FETH Remains Stable
data.description: According to BlockBeats, monitoring data from Farside revealed a net outflow of $113.8 million in FBTC yesterday. Meanwhile, FETH experienced no net inflow or outflow, indicating stability in its movement.

data.title: Solana's Market Position Strengthens Amidst DEX Volume Surge
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Solana's native token, SOL, has faced challenges in maintaining its bullish momentum after reaching $134 on April 14. Despite being 57% down from its all-time high, analysts suggest that the altcoin's rally may not be over. The decline in decentralized applications (DApps) activity has contributed to the price drop, but the growth in deposits on the Solana network is seen as a potential catalyst for short-term price increases.

Solana has emerged as the second-largest blockchain by total value locked (TVL), reaching $6.9 billion. Over the seven days ending April 16, Solana gained 12%, surpassing competitors like Tron, Base, and Berachain. Notable developments include a 30% increase in deposits on Sanctum, a liquid staking application, and a 20% growth on Jito and Jupiter. Solana's decentralized exchange (DEX) volume has also outpaced Ethereum's layer-2 solutions, with trading activity totaling $15.8 billion, exceeding Ethereum's by over 50% during the same period.

Solana reclaimed the top spot in DEX activity, surpassing Ethereum after a 16% gain over seven days. This was driven by a 44% increase in volume on Pump-fun and a 28% rise on Raydium. In contrast, volumes on Ethereum's largest DApps—Uniswap, Fluid, and Curve Finance—declined. A similar trend was observed on the BNB Chain, where PancakeSwap, Four-Meme, and DODO experienced reduced volumes compared to the previous week. Solana's growth is not limited to DEX performance, as other DApps like Ondo Finance and Exponent have also shown significant increases in total value locked.

Analysts are optimistic about the potential approval of a Solana spot exchange-traded fund (ETF) in the United States in 2025. However, expectations for substantial inflows are tempered by a general lack of interest from institutional investors and the recent underperformance of similar Ethereum ETF instruments. If approved, the spot ETF could bolster Solana's presence, particularly if the U.S. government's Digital Asset Stockpile plans are realized. Investors are also awaiting the audit of U.S. federal agencies' crypto holdings, initially expected by April 7. Despite missing this deadline, there are no current plans for the government to acquire cryptocurrencies other than Bitcoin.

While there are few catalysts to justify a rally to $180, a level last seen on March 2, the increase in TVL and DEX market share alone may not be sufficient to drive SOL's price to outperform the broader market. External factors and a large influx of new participants into the crypto ecosystem would be necessary for such a price surge.

data.title: Gate.io WCTC S7 Participants Exceed 10,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Gate.io WCTC S7 has opened early bird registration on April 15. Within 48 hours of registration opening, the number of participating users exceeded 10,000, successfully unlocking a prize pool of 140,000 US dollars. It is reported that this competition covers a series of activities such as team competitions, individual competitions, and red envelope rain, with a dynamic prize pool of up to 5 million US dollars. The registration time is from 00:00 on April 15 to 00:00 on June 8 (UTC), and the competition time is from 00:00 on May 7 to 00:00 on June 8 (UTC).

data.title: Second Round of Binance Delisting Vote Ends
data.description: The second round of Binance’s delisting vote has concluded. The top five projects by vote share were FTT (11.1%), ZEC (8.6%), JASMY (8.6%), GPS (8.2%), and PDA (7.6%).
